title: Platystele caudatisepala
taxonomy: Platystele caudatisepala is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Platystele, which is a group of epiphytic orchids found in Central and South America. The specific epithet 'caudatisepala' suggests that this species has distinctive caudate (tail-like) sepals. The species is further classified as a member of the tribe Epidendreae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. Platystele caudatisepala is considered to be an endemic species, meaning it is found only in a specific geographic region.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
